bonuses.leadin: Answer some questions about the film career of actress Catherine Deneuve. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer some questions about the film career of actress Catherine Deneuve. For 10 points each:
parts: Deneuve is most famous for her collaborations with Jacques Demy during this cinematic movement. In a film from this movement, a Humphrey Bogart-obsessed character kills a cop after being caught with a stolen car.

Deneuve plays a woman who turns to prostitution to fulfill masochistic fantasies in this director’s film Belle de Jour. This man worked with Salvador Dali in his debut, Un Chien Andalou (“ahn SHYEN ahn-DAH-loo”).

In a film inspired by the Dogme (“DAW-muh”) 95 movement, Deneuve plays a woman who describes Hollywood musicals to a blind character played by this actress. This actress secured an Academy Award nomination for her song “I’ve Seen It All.”

answers: French New Wave [accept nouvelle vague] (The unnamed film is Breathless.)

Rouch’s Moi, un Noir (“MWAH uhn nwah”) was cited as inspiring this French film movement through its use of jump-cuts and non-professional actors. Other directors from this 1950s movement included François Truffaut (”frahn-SWAH troo-FOH”).

Rouch took heavy inspiration from this director for the filming theory of Chronicle of a Summer. Rouch’s style of “cinéma vérité” (“VEH-ree-teh”) was coined by his partner Edgar Morin when referring to this Russian director of Man with a Movie Camera.

bonuses.leadin: This film’s protagonist obsesses over a memory of seeing an unnamed woman at Orly Airport shortly before witnessing the death of his adult self. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this 1962 science fiction film directed by Chris Marker (“mar-KAIR”). This film is comprised almost exclusively of still photographs accompanied by voiceover dialogue and narration.
Marker was often grouped with the Left Bank directors of this French film movement along with Alain Resnais (“ah-LAN reh-NAY”) and husband-and-wife Jacques Demy and Agnés Varda.
In contrast, the Right Bank included more popular and successful filmmakers such as this director of Pierrot le fou and Vivre sa vie, both of which star his then-wife Anna Karina.

parts: Name this profession. A member of this profession named Wallas murders Daniel Dupont and searches for one of the title objects in Alain Robbe-Grillet's novel The Erasers.

Modiano's Missing Person won this most prestigious award in French literature in 1978. The male-dominated juries that decided this award in its early years inspired the creation of the Prix Femina, whose jury consists entirely of women.

Robbe-Grillet's The Erasers is a major example of an experimental style of French novel described by this adjective, a phrase coined by Émile Henriot. Robbe-Grillet also collaborated with Alain Resnais, a director associated with a French film “wave” described by this adjective.

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about the wide world of New Wave film, for 10 points each.
parts: Handheld cameras, natural lighting and rebellious protagonists characterize many of the films in this country’s New Wave movement, which produced Breathless and The 400 Blows.

Films from this country’s recent New Wave include one in which the protagonist argues over the meaning of the words “conscience” and “justice” called Police, Adjective, as well as a film asking if this country’s revolution actually affected a small town, titled “12:08 East of” its capital.
The Asian financial crisis spurred this country’s New Wave cinema, which produced such art-house films as Last Life in the Universe and the spaghetti western-esque Tears of the Black Tiger.
